svg.title: ANE-RE research overview
svg.desc: Apple Neural Engine reverse engineering and research overview with verified metrics, capabilities, public research frontier, and the ANE-RE field engineer.
svg.text: Apple Neural Engine Reverse Engineering & Research
Understand the hardware. Build the future of local intelligence.
Pushing the frontier of what is possible on Apple Silicon.

text.tspan: ANE-RE authors graphs directly, compiles them through Apple’s public toolchain,
and measures what actually executes on the Neural Engine.
Native Swift tooling, controlled experiments, preserved negative results, and separate gates
for placement, correctness, stability, and performance.

svg.text: Hand-authored 28-layer LUT4 Qwen2.5-1.5B runs on the Apple Neural Engine
Direct SwiftMIL authoring · public Core ML compiler path · full WikiText-2 validation

g.text: SwiftMIL, deterministic printing, packages and weights
shape, layout, quantization and toolchain probes
real H17 .hwx extraction and structural decoding
KV reads, writes, continuity and update behavior
dispatch, compute, memory and steady-state cost
scoped SwiftMIL graphs reached full placement
read, write and update paths measured on-device
byte-exact independence in the validated substrate
close batch, quantization and state interaction
turn verified K>1 execution into a fair speed win
extend the method across more architectures
grow the catalog; keep strategic recipes private

svg.text: ANE-RE · Built with Swift · Measured on A18 Pro / H17 · Led by Nayte Wilson
